Some specialty elements can be integrated into standard 30-day programs, while others (like our long-term program) extend for 60-90+ days. Our trauma and family programs often require 45-60 days for optimal outcomes.</p> </div> </div> </div> <div class="accordion-row"> <div class="relative flex items-center justify-between accordion-title js-accordion-title"> <div class="accordion-title__text"> <h4>Do specialty programs cost more than regular treatment?</h4> </div> <div class="accordion-title__plusicon"> <span> <i> <svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"> <g clip-path="url(#clip0_2308_11349)"> <path d="M6.75742 11.4675L0.307861 5.01777C-0.102621 4.60748 -0.102621 3.94228 0.307861 3.5322C0.717978 3.12208 1.38315 3.12208 1.79323 3.5322L7.50011 9.23924L13.2068 3.53236C13.6171 3.12225 14.2822 3.12225 14.6923 3.53236C15.1026 3.94248 15.1026 4.60765 14.6923 5.01793L8.24263 11.4677C8.03747 11.6728 7.76887 11.7752 7.50014 11.7752C7.23128 11.7752 6.96248 11.6726 6.75742 11.4675Z" fill="white"/> </g> <defs> <clipPath id="clip0_2308_11349"> <rect width="15" height="15" fill="white" transform="translate(15) rotate(90)"/> </clipPath> </defs> </svg> </i> </span> </div> <div class="hidden accordion-title__minusicon"> <span> <i> <svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"> <g clip-path="url(#clip0_2308_11101)"> <path d="M8.24258 3.53247L14.6921 9.98223C15.1026 10.3925 15.1026 11.0577 14.6921 11.4678C14.282 11.8779 13.6169 11.8779 13.2068 11.4678L7.49989 5.76076L1.79321 11.4676C1.38293 11.8778 0.717829 11.8778 0.307712 11.4676C-0.102571 11.0575 -0.102571 10.3924 0.307712 9.98207L6.75737 3.53231C6.96253 3.32725 7.23113 3.22484 7.49986 3.22484C7.76872 3.22484 8.03752 3.32745 8.24258 3.53247Z" fill="white"/> </g> <defs> <clipPath id="clip0_2308_11101"> <rect width="15" height="15" fill="white" transform="translate(0 15) rotate(-90)"/> </clipPath> </defs> </svg> </i> </span> </div> </div> <div class="accordion-content"> <div class="content"> <p>Specialty program costs vary based on length and intensity of care. staff have additional training and certifications specific to their specialty areas. This includes trauma specialists, chaplains, family therapists, and clinicians with experience working with specific populations like first responders or faith communities.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </section> <section class="img-with-content relative overflow-hidden lg:py-[90px] md:py-60 py-55 z-1" > <div class="pattern absolute top-0 left-0 w-full h-full -z-1 bg-white"> <img src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E" alt="Soothing background image of white and gray waves." class="w-full h-full block object-cover" data-lazy-src="https://www.beachway.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/AdobeStock_318195736-scaled.jpg">

 Skip to content

 Comprehensive Specialty Rehab Programs 
 in West Palm Beach 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 Unlocking Healing Through Cultural Competence and Clinical Excellence 
 
Your path to recovery is as unique as your story, your heritage, and your faith. At Beachway, we believe that true healing happens when we honor all aspects of who you are: not just the symptoms you’re facing, but the cultural identity, spiritual beliefs, and life experiences that shape your worldview.
 
Recovery isn’t just about overcoming addiction or trauma. It’s about reclaiming your whole self and discovering how your cultural strengths can become the foundation for lasting transformation.
 
 Clinical Excellence Rooted in Cultural Understanding 
 
Our approach combines evidence-based clinical care with deep cultural competence. Our licensed clinicians specialize in trauma, mental health, and substance use disorders, but they also understand something crucial: your cultural identity isn’t separate from your healing- it’s central to it.
 
Whether you’re a veteran carrying the weight of service, someone whose faith provides meaning and direction, or a person whose ethnic heritage shapes your values and worldview, we see your cultural identity as a source of strength, not something to set aside during treatment.
 
 
 Schedule Your Personalized Rehab Program Today 
 
 
 
 
 
 What makes our approach different: 
 
 Trauma-informed care that considers cultural trauma and intergenerational experiences
 
 Faith-integrated treatment for those who find strength in spiritual practices
 
 Community-based healing that honors the role of family, tribe, and cultural connection
 
 Specialized tracks designed for specific populations and their unique needs
 
 The Power of Cultural Identity in Recovery 
 
Many traditional treatment programs ask you to leave parts of yourself at the door. We invite you to bring your whole self into the healing process. Here’s why this matters:
 
 Your Heritage as a Resource : The values, traditions, and wisdom passed down through your family and community aren’t obstacles to overcome. They’re tools for healing. Whether it’s the resilience traditions of your ancestors, the spiritual practices that have sustained your people, or the community values that define your identity, these become active parts of your recovery.
 
 Shared Experience and Understanding : When you’re surrounded by others who share similar backgrounds, challenges, or values, healing happens more naturally. The veteran who understands the weight of service, the person of faith who shares your spiritual language, or the community member who knows your cultural struggles: these connections create space for authentic healing.
 
 Integration, Not Replacement : We don’t ask you to choose between your cultural identity and your recovery. Instead, we help you integrate these aspects of yourself, showing you how your heritage can support and strengthen your journey to wellness.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
Explore Our Range of Specialty Rehab Programs
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
Satori; Holistic 
Wellness Program
 
 
 
SATORI – Science and Transcendental Orientation for Recovery and Insight. An advanced spirituality course for individuals seeking deep insight. The word Satori in Zen Buddhism means a sudden awakening or deep insight into one’s true nature. In this program, SATORI symbolizes the meeting point of science and spirituality. We integrate neuroscience, psychology, and evidence-based recovery practices with timeless spiritual wisdom and contemplative traditions. This orientation helps participants cultivate clarity, self-awareness, and resilience while moving beyond suffering. The aim is not just recovery, but awakening to a life of freedom, balance, and purpose.
 
 Satori Program Features: 
 
Understand the brain’s role in addiction and healing
 
Develop skills for emotional regulation and resilience
 
Explore spiritual principles that foster meaning and freedom
 
Experience practices that cultivate clarity, balance, and self-awareness
 
 Clinical Approach: The goal of SATORI is not only recovery from suffering but the discovery of a deeper sense of purpose and awakening.
 
 
 Holistic Satori Program 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
Christian Treatment Center 
with Clinical Excellence
 
 
 
Our faith-based program integrates spiritual beliefs with evidence-based addiction and mental health treatment, recognizing that spirituality can be a powerful tool in recovery for those who value their faith.
 
 Faith-Based Program Features: 
 
Biblical counseling alongside clinical therapy and psychiatric care
 
Christian recovery groups and spiritual mentorship opportunities
 
Chaplain support and spiritual care planning
 
Faith-based coping strategies for addiction recovery and mental health
 
Community connection with local faith organizations
 
Integration of Christian principles with clinical best practices
 
 Clinical Excellence: Our faith-based program maintains the same clinical standards as our other programs, with licensed therapists, psychiatric care, and evidence-based treatments. Faith integration enhances rather than replaces proven clinical approaches.
 
 Dual Diagnosis Focus: We address mental health conditions within a faith context, helping patients understand that depression, anxiety, and other conditions are medical issues that can be treated alongside spiritual growth.
 
 
 Learn About Our Christian Treatment Program 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
Kosher Jewish 
Integrated Program
 
 
 
The Jewish Faith-Based Track is integrated into the wider treatment program at Florida Addiction Treatment Center. Our center provides a full spectrum of individualized, evidence-based care for substance use and co-occurring mental health conditions.
 
 Program Features: 
 
Extended stabilization for complex dual diagnosis conditions
 
Treatment for chronic relapse patterns with intensive relapse prevention
 
Gradual reintegration planning and comprehensive life skills development
 
Enhanced family therapy and relationship rebuilding over time
 
Community connection with local faith organizations
 
Comprehensive aftercare and alumni support systems
 
Therapy groups integrating Jewish texts
 
Access to Kosher groceries and Kosher catered meals
 
 Benefits: Services include medical detox, partial hospitalization, and intensive outpatient options, along with supportive housing that features 24/7 supervision and transportation.
 
 Proven Outcomes: Clients also have access to psychiatric and medical care, structured aftercare planning, and a strong alumni community that fosters long-term recovery.
 
 
 Kosher Treatment Program 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
Family Recovery and 
Healing Services
 
 
 
Addiction affects entire family systems, often creating secondary trauma, enabling behaviors, and damaged relationships that require specialized intervention. Our family program addresses these systemic issues while supporting the primary patient's recovery.
 
 Family Program Features: 
 
Comprehensive family therapy addressing addiction's impact on relationships
 
Education about addiction and mental health as medical conditions
 
Communication skills and boundary setting training
 
Secondary trauma treatment for family members
 
Al-Anon and family support group facilitation
 
Couples therapy and relationship rebuilding strategies
 
 Family Healing Approach: We recognize that families often develop unhealthy patterns around addiction and mental health. Our program helps families heal these patterns while learning how to support recovery without enabling.
 
 Evidence-Based Benefits: Research shows that family involvement in treatment improves recovery outcomes by up to 60%, making family therapy an essential component of comprehensive care.
 
 
 Get Support with Family Recovery 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
First Responders Specialized Treatment Program
 
 
 
First responders face unique challenges including occupational trauma, PTSD, high-stress environments, and cultural barriers to seeking help. Our specialized program addresses these specific needs with clinicians who understand first responder culture.
 
 First Responder Program Features: 
 
Treatment for occupational PTSD and traumatic stress
 
Understanding of first responder culture and barriers to treatment
 
Specialized groups for police, firefighters, EMS, and military personnel
 
Career counseling and return-to-work planning
 
Family therapy addressing the impact of first responder stress on families
 
Confidential treatment protecting career and security clearances
 
 Cultural Competence: Our staff includes former first responders and clinicians with extensive experience treating this population, ensuring cultural understanding and trust-building essential for treatment success.
 
 
 Support for First Responders in Recovery 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
Comprehensive Trauma 
Recovery Program
 
 
 
Our specialized trauma track addresses the complex relationship between trauma and addiction, recognizing that up to 90% of individuals seeking addiction treatment have trauma histories. This program provides intensive, specialized care for trauma survivors who need more than standard addiction treatment.
 
 Trauma Program Features: 
 
EMDR, psychodrama, and somatic therapies led by trauma specialists
 
Gender-specific trauma groups for enhanced safety and healing
 
Treatment for PTSD, complex trauma, childhood abuse, and sexual trauma
 
Family therapy addressing secondary trauma and relationship healing
 
Specialized protocols for trauma survivors in early recovery
 
Integration of trauma healing with addiction recovery goals
 
 Clinical Approach: Our trauma program uses a three-phase approach: stabilization and safety, trauma processing, and integration and reconnection. This evidence-based model ensures patients build strong foundations before processing difficult memories and experiences.
 
 
 Trauma Recovery Program 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
Comprehensive Assessment for Specialty Program Placement
 
Not everyone needs specialty care, but for those who do, proper assessment and placement are crucial for treatment success. Our clinical team conducts thorough evaluations to determine appropriate specialty care needs.
 
Detailed Clinical Assessment Process
 
 Assessment Components Include: 
 
 
 Comprehensive trauma history and PTSD evaluation 

 Cultural, spiritual, and religious needs assessment 

 Family system assessment and involvement readiness 

 Mental health complexity and dual diagnosis identification 
 
 Previous treatment history and relapse pattern analysis 

 Specialized population considerations (first responders, military, etc.) 

 Occupational and educational background evaluation 

 Social support system and environmental factors 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Individualized Treatment Planning: Based on assessment results, our team develops personalized treatment plans that may include one or multiple specialty tracks, ensuring each patient receives the most appropriate level and type of care.
 
Matching Patients to Appropriate Specialty Programs
 
 Clinical Indicators for Specialty Care: 
 
 
 Trauma Program: History of PTSD, complex trauma, childhood abuse, or sexual trauma 

 Faith-Based Program: Strong religious beliefs and desire for spiritual integration 

 Long-Term Program: Multiple treatment failures, complex dual diagnosis, or chronic relapse 
 
 Family Program: Significant family dysfunction, secondary trauma, or relationship breakdown 

 First Responder Program: Occupational trauma, PTSD from emergency service work 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Flexible Program Participation: Many patients benefit from combining elements of multiple specialty programs. Our flexible approach allows for individualized treatment plans that address all relevant specialty needs.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 Community-Centered Healing 
 
Recovery thrives in community, and authentic community honors who you really are. In our culturally-focused groups, you’ll find:
 
 Safe Spaces for Identity Exploration : Optional groups designed for specific populations provide a place to explore how your cultural background has shaped both your struggles and your strengths. These aren’t just support groups: they’re spaces for cultural reclamation and empowerment.
 
 Storytelling as Medicine : When you share your story with others who understand your context, something powerful happens. The shame that often accompanies addiction or trauma begins to lift as you realize your struggles make sense within your cultural experience, and your resilience becomes a source of inspiration for others.
 
 Cultural Wisdom in Action : Group discussions naturally weave together clinical insights with cultural wisdom. You might explore how your grandmother’s advice about perseverance applies to recovery, or how your military training in mission focus translates to recovery goals.
 
 Building Your Chosen Family : For many, treatment becomes a place to build new community connections while honoring existing ones. You’ll find people who understand your journey and can walk alongside you long after treatment ends.
 
 
 
 Specialized Tracks for Unique Journeys 
 
We offer specialized treatment tracks designed for specific communities, each addressing unique challenges and drawing on specific cultural strengths:
 
 
 Veteran and First Responder Track Designed for those who've served others, addressing service-related trauma, PTSD, moral injury, and the challenge of civilian reintegration. We honor your service while helping you heal from its costs. 

 Christian Track For those whose faith in Jesus Christ provides meaning and direction, integrating biblical principles with clinical care. Explore themes of grace, redemption, and identity in Christ as foundational to your recovery. 

 Orthodox Jewish Track Culturally sensitive care that honors Jewish traditions, observances, and values. We provide kosher meals, Rabbinic consultation, Shabbos observance, and integrate Jewish concepts of healing and community into your treatment. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Cultural Rituals and Practices 
 
Beyond group therapy, we offer optional cultural and spiritual practices that help you reconnect with your heritage:
 
 Sacred Practices : Whether it’s prayer, meditation, ceremony, or ritual, we create space for the spiritual practices that ground you and connect you to something larger than yourself.
 
 Community Celebrations : Observing holidays, traditions, and meaningful dates within your cultural calendar, helping you stay connected to your roots while building new, healthy associations.
 
 Mentorship and Elder Wisdom : Connection with others who share your background and have walked similar paths, providing guidance rooted in shared understanding and cultural wisdom.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 How Our Specialty Tracks Work 
 
Our specialty tracks are thoughtfully woven into our comprehensive treatment program, creating space for cultural connection while maintaining the benefits of our diverse therapeutic community. Rather than separating clients into isolated groups, these tracks operate as enriching additions to your core treatment experience.
 
You’ll participate in the full range of our clinical programming alongside clients from all backgrounds, building relationships across different communities and experiences. Within this inclusive environment, your specialty track provides dedicated time and space to connect with others who share your cultural background, faith tradition, or service experience. These focused sessions allow you to explore identity-specific themes, practice cultural or spiritual rituals, and draw strength from shared understanding, all while remaining part of our broader healing community.
 
This integrated approach ensures you receive comprehensive clinical care while having access to the cultural competence and peer support that can make recovery more meaningful and sustainable. You’re not separated from others, but you do have additional resources tailored to your specific journey.
 
 
 
 
 
 Individual results may vary. Success rates based on internal program data and follow-up surveys conducted 6-12 months post-treatment. 
 
Why Specialty Care Works Better
 
 Targeted Treatment Approaches: Specialty programs address specific risk factors and challenges that generic programs may miss, leading to more effective treatment outcomes.
 
 Cultural Competence: Understanding the unique cultural, occupational, or spiritual context of different populations builds trust and engagement essential for recovery success.
 
 Peer Support: Being in treatment with others who share similar experiences creates stronger peer connections and mutual understanding that supports long-term recovery.
 
 Extended Focus: Specialty programs often allow for longer or more intensive treatment of specific issues, providing the time needed for deep healing and skill development.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 Your Whole Self, Your Whole Healing 
 
At Beachway, we’re committed to seeing you as a complete person: not just someone with a diagnosis or a problem to fix. Your cultural identity, spiritual beliefs, family history, and life experiences all matter in your recovery journey.
 
We believe that when you can bring your authentic self to treatment, when your cultural strengths are recognized and celebrated, and when you’re surrounded by community that understands your journey, healing becomes not just possible, but inevitable.
 
 Your recovery is personal. Your treatment should be too. 
 
Ready to begin a recovery journey that honors who you are? Connect with us to learn more about our culturally-focused specialty tracks and discover how your heritage can become your greatest asset in healing.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

Frequently Asked Questions about Specialty Programs
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
How do you determine if I need a specialty program?
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Our clinical assessment team evaluates your background, trauma history, cultural needs, and treatment goals to determine if specialty care would benefit your recovery. This includes reviewing previous treatment attempts, current mental health needs, and specific population factors.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Can I participate in multiple specialty tracks?
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Yes, many patients benefit from combining elements of different specialty programs. For example, a first responder with trauma history might participate in both our trauma program and first responder program. We customize treatment plans to address all relevant specialty needs.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
What makes faith-based treatment different from standard rehab?
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Our faith-based program integrates spiritual beliefs and practices with the same evidence-based clinical treatments used in our other programs. It includes religious counseling, spiritual direction, and commmunity support alongside licensed therapy, psychiatric care, and proven addiction treatment methods.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
How long are specialty programs compared to standard treatment?
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Specialty program length varies based on individual needs. Some specialty elements can be integrated into standard 30-day programs, while others (like our long-term program) extend for 60-90+ days. Our trauma and family programs often require 45-60 days for optimal outcomes.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Do specialty programs cost more than regular treatment?
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Specialty program costs vary based on length and intensity of care. Many insurance plans cover specialty care when medically necessary. Our financial team works with patients to understand coverage and payment options for specialty treatments.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
What qualifications do specialty program staff have?
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Our specialty program staff have additional training and certifications specific to their specialty areas. This includes trauma specialists, chaplains, family therapists, and clinicians with experience working with specific populations like first responders or faith communities.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
Start Your Specialized Recovery Journey Today
 
At Beachway Therapy Center, we believe that everyone deserves treatment that addresses their unique needs, background, and recovery goals. Our specialty programs provide the individualized care necessary for lasting recovery from both addiction and mental health conditions.
 
 Why Choose Beachway for Specialty Care: 
 
Evidence-based specialty programs with proven outcomes
 
Integrated addiction and mental health treatment in all programs
 
Culturally competent staff with specialized training
 
Beautiful West Palm Beach facility designed for optimal healing
 
Comprehensive aftercare and alumni support
 
Joint Commission accreditation and state licensing
 
 Take the Next Step: Recovery is possible, and specialized care can make the difference between treatment that helps temporarily and treatment that creates lasting change. Contact us today to learn more about our specialty programs and how they can support your unique recovery journey.
 
 
 Schedule Your Personalized Rehab Program Today

At Beachway, we monitor each patient individually using evidence-based protocols and may provide medication-assisted withdrawal management to safely detox the body while alleviating both physical withdrawal symptoms and psychological distress.

Our detox treatment center has licensed medical professionals and psychiatric specialists available to supervise patients during the withdrawal process while addressing the mental health aspects that often emerge during early recovery.
Licensed healthcare professionals also monitor each patient’s vital signs, including heart rate, blood pressure, and neurological status, to ensure their bodies tolerate the detox process.

Frequently Asked Questions About Dual Diagnosis Treatment
How long does dual diagnosis treatment take?
Dual diagnosis treatment typically requires 60-90 days for optimal outcomes, as both mental health and addiction conditions need adequate time to stabilize and integrate.
Will my insurance cover dual diagnosis treatment?
Most insurance plans cover dual diagnosis treatment when it's medically necessary.
Our admissions team verifies coverage, helps with prior authorization, and works with insurance companies to ensure appropriate coverage for dual diagnosis care.
Medication compliance is often essential for dual diagnosis recovery success.
What makes dual diagnosis treatment different from regular rehab?

Dual diagnosis treatment involves specialized staff trained in both addiction and mental health, integrated therapy approaches that address both conditions simultaneously, psychiatric care throughout treatment, and careful medication management.
What happens if I relapse during dual diagnosis treatment?

Yes, we treat all common dual diagnosis combinations including depression and substance abuse, anxiety and addiction, PTSD and substance use disorders, bipolar disorder and addiction, and many others.
How involved will my family be in dual diagnosis treatment?
Family involvement is crucial for dual diagnosis recovery success.
What support is available after dual diagnosis treatment ends?

evidence.snippet: Dual diagnosis treatment typically requires 60-90 days for optimal outcomes, as both mental health and addiction conditions need adequate time to stabilize and integrate.

First responders face unique challenges including occupational trauma, PTSD, high-stress environments, and cultural barriers to seeking help.

Cultural Competence: Our staff includes former first responders and clinicians with extensive experience treating this population, ensuring cultural understanding and trust-building essential for treatment success.
Support for First Responders in Recovery
Specialized population considerations (first responders, military, etc.
This includes trauma specialists, chaplains, family therapists, and clinicians with experience working with specific populations like first responders or faith communities.
